message:
test(mvcc,alloc,dir,journal): add proptest property-based tests + clippy fix

Add 29 new proptest property-based tests across four crates:

- ffs-mvcc (8 tests): committed writes visibility, snapshot isolation,
  version chain monotonicity, FCW conflict detection, non-overlapping
  concurrent writes, sequential last-value-wins, unwritten block returns
  None, abort discards writes

- ffs-alloc (15 tests): SuccinctBitmap rank1/select1 roundtrip,
  rank0/select0 roundtrip, rank1 monotonicity, ones+zeros=len, find_free
  returns zero bit, find_contiguous valid run; bitmap set/get roundtrip,
  count_free consistency, find_free returns zero, find_contiguous valid,
  alloc/free roundtrip preserves free count, multi-block alloc/free
  roundtrip, alloc marks bitmap bits, alloc no overlaps, find_contiguous
  wraparound valid

- ffs-dir (6 tests): add/remove state machine vs reference HashMap,
  remove-then-add space reclamation, htree insert maintains sorted order,
  htree find_leaf rightmost-lte, htree remove/insert roundtrip, dx_hash
  determinism + bit-0 convention

Also fixes clippy unnecessary_map_or lint in ffs-journal (map_or →
is_none_or) and includes adversarial journal replay tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1fo","title":"btrfs: Implement node parse helpers (internal + leaf, item table bounds)","description":"Goal: provide safe parsing primitives for btrfs tree nodes.

Deliverables:
- Parse node header (already exists) and validate bytenr, nritems, level.
- Parse item table for:
  - leaf nodes (items point to payload within block)
  - internal nodes (items point to child block pointers)
- Provide iterators that yield keys + (offset,size) or key + child pointer.

Acceptance:
- Unit tests cover malformed item tables (overlaps, out-of-bounds offsets, overflow).

 {"id":"bd-1j8z","title":"S3-FIFO invariant panic hardening in ffs-block","description":"Replace panic-on-drift behavior in S3-FIFO hit/miss/rebalance paths with deterministic self-healing so transient queue-map inconsistencies do not abort process. Keep capacity and correctness invariants while preserving perf gates.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:19:17.265664366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T06:25:00.503790453Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T06:25:00.503767510Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["block","s3fifo","stability"],"comments":[{"id":278,"issue_id":"bd-1j8z","author":"SunnyElm","text":"Implemented S3-FIFO invariant panic hardening in crates/ffs-block/src/lib.rs. Replaced panic-on-drift behavior in S3 hit/miss and overflow handling with deterministic recovery: (1) hit-path repairs stale ghost/missing loc metadata back into resident queues, (2) miss-path drops stale resident metadata instead of triggering debug-assert panic path, (3) overflow path runs bounded clean-eviction recovery and overflow-only queue reconciliation, then logs invariant violation without aborting process. Added reconciliation guard for pending admissions to preserve miss-before-insert lifecycle and avoid false drops. Validation: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --workspace --all-targets; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p ffs-block --all-features --lib; cargo test -p ffs-block --all-features --test perf_regression_e2e -- --nocapture; cargo test --workspace --all-features all pass. UBS scan on changed file now reports 2 critical (down from 5): remaining panics are dirty-eviction guard rails.","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:24:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1mdk","title":"EPIC: Bw-Trees for Extent Indexes (Graveyard Entry 8.7)","description":"# EPIC: Bw-Trees for Extent Indexes (Graveyard Entry 8.7)

## PURPOSE
Replace traditional B-tree extent indexes with Bw-Trees (Buzzword Trees) — lock-free delta-chain trees that allow concurrent readers and writers without any reader blocking. This is a long-term performance optimization for the extent index hot path.

## BACKGROUND
From the Alien CS Graveyard entry 8.7:
- Bw-Tree (Levandoski et al., ICDE 2013): latch-free B-tree with delta chains
- Key insight: instead of in-place mutation, append delta records to page
- Readers never block: they traverse the delta chain from newest to oldest
- Writers use CAS to append deltas (no locks)
- Periodic consolidation merges delta chains back into base pages

## DESIGN

### Architecture
- Mapping table: PageId -> AtomicPtr<PageDelta>
- Delta chain: linked list of delta records (insert, delete, split, merge)
- Base page: consolidated B-tree node
- Consolidation: background thread merges delta chains when they exceed threshold

### Delta Records
```rust
enum PageDelta {
    Insert { key: ExtentKey, value: ExtentValue, next: *const PageDelta },
    Delete { key: ExtentKey, next: *const PageDelta },
    Split { split_key: ExtentKey, new_page_id: PageId, next: *const PageDelta },
    Merge { removed_page_id: PageId, next: *const PageDelta },
    Base(BTreeNode),
}
```

### Concurrency
- Lookup: traverse mapping table -> delta chain -> base page (all read-only, no locks)
- Insert/Delete: create delta, CAS into mapping table (retry on conflict)
- Split/Merge: structural modification via delta + CAS
- Consolidation: create new base page, CAS to replace delta chain

### Extent Index Integration
- Replace extent B-tree in ffs-ext4 and ffs-btrfs with Bw-Tree
- Same lookup API: find_extent(inode, logical_block) -> PhysicalExtent
- Same mutation API: insert_extent, delete_extent, split_extent

## CURRENT PRIORITY
P2 — performance optimization, not blocking correctness. Current B-tree works correctly.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Lock-free lookup: readers never block on writers
- [ ] CAS-based insert/delete: no mutex on hot path
- [ ] Delta chain consolidation prevents unbounded chain growth
- [ ] Benchmark: lookup throughput under concurrent writers vs B-tree with locks
- [ ] Correctness: all B-tree invariants maintained (sorted, balanced, split/merge correct)
- [ ] Drop-in replacement for current extent index

## Success Criteria
1. All children closed
2. E2E benchmark (bd-1mdk.4) shows improvement over locked B-tree
3. Unit tests (bd-1mdk.3) pass
4. Delta chain consolidation compacts correctly
5. Mapping table is lock-free (no mutex in hot path)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:25:46.207227957Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T00:20:07.541669980Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T00:20:07.541602964Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bwtree","index","lockfree"],"comments":[{"id":98,"issue_id":"bd-1mdk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW (lint fix): Epic is missing ## Success Criteria section. Add measurable success criteria before any children are marked closed. Example format: '## Success Criteria\n1. All children closed\n2. E2E test suite passes\n3.  {"id":"bd-1mdk.1","title":"Implement Bw-Tree mapping table and delta chain core","description":"# Implement Bw-Tree mapping table and delta chain core

## GOAL
Implement the foundational data structures for a Bw-Tree (Levandoski et al., ICDE 2013): the mapping table (PageId -> AtomicPtr<PageDelta>) and delta chain (linked list of insert/delete/split/merge deltas appended via CAS).

## BACKGROUND
From Alien CS Graveyard entry 8.7: Bw-Trees are latch-free B-trees that replace in-place mutation with append-only delta records. Readers traverse the delta chain from newest to oldest, never blocking. Writers use compare-and-swap (CAS) to atomically append deltas. This eliminates all reader-side synchronization.

## DESIGN

### Mapping Table
```rust
struct MappingTable {
    pages: Vec<AtomicPtr<PageDelta>>,
    next_page_id: AtomicU64,
}
```
- Fixed-size array of atomic pointers (one per page/node)
- All B-tree operations go through mapping table indirection
- Enables lock-free structural modifications (split/merge update a pointer, not a tree)

### Delta Chain
```rust
enum PageDelta {
    Insert { key: ExtentKey, value: ExtentValue, next: *const PageDelta },
    Delete { key: ExtentKey, next: *const PageDelta },
    Split { separator: ExtentKey, new_sibling: PageId, next: *const PageDelta },
    Merge { removed_sibling: PageId, next: *const PageDelta },
    Base(BTreeNode),
}
```
- Each delta is a small record prepended to the chain via CAS
- Lookup: walk chain applying deltas to reconstruct current state
- Base page: consolidated snapshot of all deltas (periodic compaction target)

### CAS Append Protocol
1. Read current head pointer from mapping table
2. Create new delta with next = current head
3. CAS mapping_table[page_id] from current to new delta
4. On CAS failure: retry (another writer appended concurrently)

### Memory Management
- Delta nodes allocated via crossbeam-epoch (same EBR as MVCC versions)
- Old deltas freed after consolidation and epoch advance
- No manual memory management or unsafe deallocation

## RUST IMPLEMENTATION NOTES
- Use `crossbeam::epoch::{Atomic, Owned, Shared, Guard}` for all pointers
- `unsafe` blocks required for Shared pointer dereference (justified by epoch pin safety)
- #[repr(C)] for delta variants to ensure stable layout
- Consider cache-line alignment for mapping table entries to avoid false sharing

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] MappingTable supports allocate_page, get_page, cas_page operations
- [ ] Delta chain supports Insert, Delete append via CAS
- [ ] Lookup correctly traverses delta chain to find current value
- [ ] CAS retry loop handles concurrent append conflicts
- [ ] crossbeam-epoch used for all delta allocation and reclamation
- [ ] Unit tests: single-threaded insert/delete/lookup round-trip
- [ ] Unit tests: concurrent CAS append (2+ threads, verify no lost updates)
- [ ] Unit tests: delta chain traversal returns correct merged state
- [ ] Logging: tracing spans for CAS attempts, retries, and chain length at lookup time","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:21:01.435985132Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T23:05:19.704819462Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T23:05:19.704794174Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bwtree","core","lockfree"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mdk.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1mdk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:21:01.435985132Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":81,"issue_id":"bd-1mdk.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW: This bead is missing a ## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS section. Every implementation bead must specify structured log events at trace/debug/info/warn/error levels with field names. ## GOAL
E2E test for ext4 Read Path Completion epic (bd-1xe). Mount a golden ext4 image, read all files and metadata, verify against known checksums.

## TEST SCENARIO
1. Mount golden ext4 fixture image (from bd-2jk.1) via FUSE read-only
2. Walk entire directory tree, verify all entries present
3. Read all files, verify BLAKE3 checksums match golden manifest
4. Verify superblock, group descriptors, inode table metadata
5. Verify journal replay produced correct state (if journal non-empty)
6. Unmount

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All files and metadata match golden checksums
2. Journal replay (if applicable) produces correct state
3. Test completes in < 30 seconds
4.  {"id":"bd-1ymg","title":"Implement structured logging with JSON output","description":"# Implement structured logging with JSON output

## GOAL
Implement production-grade structured logging for operational visibility and debugging.

## BACKGROUND
From AGENTS.md: "Deterministic audit logs for explainability."
From spec: "Evidence ledger (what evidence drove what decision)."

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Structured Log Events
```rust
#[derive(Serialize)]
pub struct LogEvent {
    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub level: Level,
    pub target: String,
    pub message: String,
    pub fields: BTreeMap<String, Value>,
    pub span: Option<SpanInfo>,
}
```

### 2. Domain-Specific Fields
```rust
// MVCC events
log_event!(level: INFO, 
    message: "transaction_commit",
    txn_id: txn.id,
    commit_seq: cs,
    write_set_size: txn.write_set.len(),
    duration_us: duration.as_micros(),
);

// Cache events
log_event!(level: DEBUG,
    message: "cache_eviction",
    block: block_num,
    reason: reason,
    t1_len: cache.t1_len(),
    t2_len: cache.t2_len(),
);
```

### 3. Span Context
```rust
let span = span!(Level::INFO, "mount", 
    image = %path, 
    mode = %if ro { "ro" } else { "rw" }
);
let _guard = span.enter();
// All logs within have span context
```

### 4. Output Formats
- Human-readable for development
- JSON for production/aggregation
- Configurable via env var or config

### 5. Log Rotation
- Size-based rotation
- Time-based rotation
- Compression of old logs

## TESTS
1. Unit: JSON output valid
2. Unit: Fields correctly serialized
3. Integration: Span context propagated
4. Integration: Rotation works

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All significant events logged
2. JSON parseable by log aggregators
3. Span context maintained
4. Performance overhead < 1%","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:56:48.749831901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T08:16:41.984506744Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T08:16:41.984484602Z","close_reason":"Implemented in crates/ffs-cli: structured tracing bootstrap (human/json), command spans/fields, and JSON logging tests; gates passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["logging","observability","ops"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ymg","depends_on_id":"bd-2jk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:20.019864660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":187,"issue_id":"bd-1ymg","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-2s4.6","title":"Add comprehensive unit tests for FUSE dispatch and lifecycle","description":"# Add comprehensive unit tests for FUSE dispatch and lifecycle

## GOAL
Provide unit test coverage for the Production FUSE Runtime epic (bd-2s4). Currently the epic has E2E tests (bd-2s4.4, bd-2s4.5, bd-3en1) but NO dedicated unit tests for the dispatch layer, lifecycle management, or errno mapping.

## TEST PLAN

### 1. Dispatch Tests
- Thread pool spawns correct number of threads
- Concurrent requests are dispatched without deadlock
- Request timeout fires and returns EIO
- Queue depth metrics are updated correctly

### 2. Lifecycle Tests
- Mount initializes all subsystems in correct order
- Unmount flushes dirty data before teardown
- SIGTERM triggers clean unmount sequence
- Double-unmount is idempotent (no panic)
- Mount with invalid image returns descriptive error

### 3. Errno Mapping Tests
- Every FfsError variant maps to correct errno
- Unknown errors map to EIO
- Cancelled maps to EINTR
- CorruptData maps to EIO
- NoSpace maps to ENOSPC
- PermissionDenied maps to EACCES

### 4. State Tests
- FsOps state is Send + Sync (compile-time test)
- Concurrent getattr calls do not race
- Cache access under lock is bounded (no starvation)

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. All tests listed above
2. Total: ~20 unit tests
3. All deterministic, no I/O (mock BlockDevice)

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Test failures log full context (operation, expected, actual)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All dispatch, lifecycle, errno, and state tests pass
2. No flaky tests (deterministic)
3. Coverage of all FUSE op errno mappings","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:41.207992681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T23:01:59.609010121Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T23:01:59.608990174Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuse","test","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:41.207992681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:49.972174278Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-cebm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:50.086927457Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":159,"issue_id":"bd-2s4.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-2s4.7","title":"Fix ext4 RW write persistence across clean unmount/remount","description":"## Summary
In privileged E2E runs, ext4 RW operations succeed, but data written in phase 3 is missing after clean unmount + RO remount.

## Repro
1. Build `ffs-cli` (or use prebuilt binary).
2. Run:
   `sudo -n env PATH="$PATH" FFS_SKIP_BUILD=1 FFS_CLI_BIN=/data/projects/frankenfs/target/release/ffs-cli FFS_AUTO_UNMOUNT=0 ./scripts/e2e/ffs_ext4_rw_smoke.sh`
3. Observe failure in phase 4: `persist.txt` is missing on RO remount.

## Evidence
- Run artifact: `artifacts/e2e/20260218_040650_ffs_ext4_rw_smoke/run.log`
- RO mount log: `artifacts/e2e/20260218_040650_ffs_ext4_rw_smoke/mount_ro_mnt_ro.log`

## Expected
Writes acknowledged in RW mount (and fsync-backed sentinel writes) remain visible after clean unmount/remount.

## Actual
`persist.txt` is readable in RW mount, but absent after remount.

## Notes
- Prior RW EIO mount failure appears resolved in this environment.
- Unmount robustness was improved in `scripts/e2e/lib.sh`, so this now consistently surfaces a persistence/durability issue rather than mount teardown noise.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"CalmGorge","created_at":"2026-02-18T09:07:52.623298157Z","created_by":"CalmGorge","updated_at":"2026-02-18T09:18:40.625109148Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T09:18:40.625071358Z","close_reason":"Completed: fixed RW persistence loss by using Apply replay mode for RW mount","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["durability","ext4","fuse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-18T09:07:52.623298157Z","created_by":"CalmGorge","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":238,"issue_id":"bd-2s4.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Resolved root cause in CLI mount path: `mount_cmd` was always opening ext4 with `Ext4JournalReplayMode::SimulateOverlay`, which made RW writes session-local and non-persistent.

Change:
- `crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs`: added `ext4_mount_replay_mode(read_write)` and switched RW mount to `Apply` while keeping RO mount on `SimulateOverlay`.
- Added unit test `ext4_mount_replay_mode_is_persistent_for_rw`.

Validation:
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p ffs-cli --all-targets` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy -p ffs-cli --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p ffs-cli --all-targets` ✅
- Privileged E2E run: `sudo -n env PATH="$PATH" FFS_SKIP_BUILD=1 FFS_CLI_BIN=/data/projects/frankenfs/target/release/ffs-cli FFS_AUTO_UNMOUNT=0 ./scripts/e2e/ffs_ext4_rw_smoke.sh`
  - Phase 4 now passes: `persist.txt` and appended `mtime-check` survive clean unmount + RO remount.
  - Artifact: `artifacts/e2e/20260218_041747_ffs_ext4_rw_smoke/run.log`.","created_at":"2026-02-18T09:18:36Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2tt","title":"Implement ext4 orphan inode recovery","description":"# Implement ext4 orphan inode recovery

## GOAL
Implement ext4 orphan inode list processing as required by FEATURE_PARITY.md: "ext4 orphan recovery parity: ❌ Not yet implemented"

## BACKGROUND
ext4 maintains an orphan inode list for crash recovery:
- Inodes being deleted but still open
- Truncated files with pending block release
- On mount, these must be cleaned up

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Orphan List Structure
```rust
/// ext4 orphan inode linked list
/// s_last_orphan in superblock -> first orphan inode
/// i_dtime in orphan inode -> next orphan (0 = end)
pub struct OrphanList {
    head: InodeNumber,
}

impl OrphanList {
    pub fn read(sb: &Ext4Superblock, device: &dyn BlockDevice) -> Result<Self>;
    pub fn iter(&self) -> OrphanIter;
}
```

### 2. Recovery Actions
```rust
pub fn recover_orphan(
    inode_num: InodeNumber,
    fs: &mut OpenFs,
    cx: &Cx,
) -> Result<OrphanAction> {
    let inode = fs.read_inode(inode_num)?;
    
    if inode.link_count == 0 {
        // Deleted file, reclaim blocks and inode
        reclaim_inode_blocks(inode_num, fs, cx)?;
        free_inode(inode_num, fs, cx)?;
        Ok(OrphanAction::Deleted)
    } else {
        // Truncated file, fix size
        truncate_to_size(inode_num, inode.size, fs, cx)?;
        Ok(OrphanAction::Truncated)
    }
}
```

### 3. Mount Integration
- Process orphan list before journal replay
- Clear orphan list after processing
- Log actions taken

### 4. Error Handling
- Invalid orphan inode -> log and skip
- Circular list -> detect and break
- I/O error -> mount read-only

## TESTS
1. Unit: Parse orphan list from superblock
2. Integration: Orphan with link_count=0 deleted
3. Integration: Orphan with link_count>0 truncated
4. Integration: Corrupt orphan list handled gracefully

## LOGGING
- Orphan list head (debug)
- Each orphan processed (info): inode, action
- Orphan list cleared (info)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All orphans processed on mount
2. Blocks correctly reclaimed
3. No orphan references remain
4. Mount succeeds after crash","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:53:00.165065314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T08:02:09.633627927Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T08:02:09.633602790Z","close_reason":"Implemented mount-time ext4 orphan recovery (delete/truncate actions + superblock orphan-state clearing), added tests, and passed rch-offloaded gates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","mount","recovery"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2tt","depends_on_id":"bd-1xe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:40.727004221Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2tt","depends_on_id":"bd-1xe.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:50:17.164101964Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2tt","depends_on_id":"bd-1xe.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:33:40.839125543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2tt","depends_on_id":"bd-huh.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:33:40.739984385Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":29,"issue_id":"bd-2tt","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FEATURE_PARITY mapping: This covers 'ext4 orphan recovery parity' (currently ❌). Maps to orphan.c. When complete: flip to ✅ in FEATURE_PARITY.md.","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:49Z"},{"id":54,"issue_id":"bd-2tt","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REVIEW FIX: Added blocks deps on bd-huh.2 (block allocation) and bd-1xe.4 (journal replay write-back). Orphan recovery is a mutation operation that frees blocks and deletes inodes. It must happen AFTER journal replay (to operate on committed state) and requires the allocator (to reclaim blocks). The mount order is: (1) journal replay write-back, (2) orphan list reading, (3) orphan recovery.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:33:46Z"},{"id":195,"issue_id":"bd-2tt","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).
